There is a unchecked return value from a function that can indicate failure downstream of function stm32_ncd_interrupt in the boards/arm/stm32h7 subsystem.
The function is located at boards/arm/stm32h7/weact-stm32h750/src/stm32_sdmmc.c:76.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

    67	/* HW Issues when not using IDMA
    68	 *
    69	 *    The FIFO lacks a FIFO data available interrupt. It only has a
    70	 * FIFO Half Full and Full interrupt. Given that the FIFO size is 64
    71	 * bytes (16 uint 32), this creates an issue for commands that read
    72	 * less than 32 bytes hereafter referred to as a Lame FIFO.
    73	 *
    74	 * The DATAEND interrupt is only issued on the FIFO being emptied and it
    75	 * appears the DTIMER does not start running again until the FIFO is read
    76	 * so that DTIMEOUT interrupt can not be used to end the read operation.
    77	 *
    78	 * The only workaround is to use the IDMA to service the FIFO and to monitor
    79	 * for a remainder in the FIFO that is less than FIFO_SIZE_IN_BYTES / 2.
    80	 *
    81	 * Therefore even when IDMA is not used, the IDMA still has to be used to
    82	 * service the FIFO to receive less than FIFO_SIZE_IN_BYTES / 2 to a local
    83	 * buffer. When the FIFO Half Full and Full interrupt do fire the FIFO will
    84	 * be monitored off the an HP work thread for a residual of less than
    85	 * FIFO_SIZE_IN_BYTES / 2.

    87	 * HW Issues when using IDMA
    88	 *
    89	 *    The DMA buffer must be located in a zone accessible via IDMA.
    90	 * For SDMMC1, IDMA cannot access SRAM123 or SRAM4. Refer to ST AN520

    46	/****************************************************************************
    47	 * Name: work_qqueue
    48	 *
    49	 * Description:
    50	 *   Queue work to be performed at a later time.  All queued work will be
    51	 *   performed on the worker thread of execution (not the caller's).
    52	 *
    53	 *   The work structure is allocated by caller, but completely managed by
    54	 *   the work queue logic.  The caller should never modify the contents of
    55	 *   the work queue structure; the caller should not call work_qqueue()
    56	 *   again until either (1) the previous work has been performed and removed
    57	 *   from the queue, or (2) work_cancel() has been called to cancel the work
    58	 *   and remove it from the work queue.
    59	 *
    60	 * Input Parameters:
    61	 *   wqueue - The work queue
    62	 *   work   - The work structure to queue
    63	 *   worker - The worker callback to be invoked.  The callback will be
    64	 *            invoked on the worker thread of execution.
    65	 *   arg    - The argument that will be passed to the worker callback when
    66	 *            int is invoked.
    67	 *   delay  - Delay (in clock ticks) from the time queue until the worker
    68	 *            is invoked. Zero means to perform the work immediately.
    69	 *
    70	 * Returned Value:
    71	 *   Zero on success, a negated errno on failure
    72	 *
    73	 ****************************************************************************/
    74	
    75	static int work_qqueue(FAR struct usr_wqueue_s *wqueue,
    76	                       FAR struct work_s *work, worker_t worker,
    77	                       FAR void *arg, clock_t delay)
    78	{
    79	  FAR struct work_s *curr;
    80	  FAR struct work_s *head;
    81	  int semcount;
    82	
    83	  /* Get exclusive access to the work queue */
    84	
    85	  while (nxmutex_lock(&wqueue->lock) < 0);
    86	
    87	  /* Initialize the work structure */
    88	
    89	  work->worker = worker;          /* Work callback. non-NULL means queued */
    90	  work->arg    = arg;             /* Callback argument */
    91	  work->qtime  = clock() + delay; /* Delay until work performed */
    92	
    93	  /* Insert the work into the wait queue sorted by the expired time. */
    94	
    95	  head = list_first_entry(&wqueue->q, struct work_s, node);
    96	
    97	  list_for_every_entry(&wqueue->q, curr, struct work_s, node)
    98	    {
    99	      if (!clock_compare(curr->qtime, work->qtime))
   100	        {
   101	          break;
   102	        }
   103	    }
   104	
   105	  /* After the insertion, we do not violate the invariant that
   106	   * the wait queue is sorted by the expired time. Because
   107	   * curr->qtime > work->qtime.
   108	   * In the case of the wqueue is empty, we insert
   109	   * the work at the head of the wait queue.
   110	   */
   111	
   112	  list_add_before(&curr->node, &work->node);
   113	
   114	  /* If the current work is the head of the wait queue.
   115	   * We should wake up the worker thread.
   116	   */
   117	
   118	  if (curr == head)
   119	    {
   120	      nxsem_get_value(&wqueue->wake, &semcount);
   121	      if (semcount < 1)
   122	        {
   123	          nxsem_post(&wqueue->wake);
   124	        }
   125	    }
   126	
   127	  nxmutex_unlock(&wqueue->lock);
   128	  return OK;
   129	}

   138	 * Description:
   139	 *   Queue user-mode work to be performed at a later time.  All queued work
   140	 *   will be performed on the worker thread of execution (not the caller's).
   141	 *
   142	 *   The work structure is allocated and must be initialized to all zero by
   143	 *   the caller.  Otherwise, the work structure is completely managed by the

   145	 *   work queue structure directly.  If work_queue() is called before the

   158	 *
